new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine indicating that it is produced exclusively from grapes of the 'Glera' vine variety.

new_classes.label_en: Prosecco protected designation of origin quality
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine indicating that it carries one of the protected designations of origin: 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', or 'Asolo — Prosecco'.

A sparkling wine (CN 2204 10) with a protected designation of origin, produced from grapes of the 'Glera' vine variety harvested in the regions bearing the designations of origin 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', or 'Asolo — Prosecco'.

coverage_explanation: All four key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) sparkling wine character (SparklingWineQuality), (2) exclusive use of Glera grape variety (GleraGrapeVarietyQuality — new class), (3) protected designation of origin from the enumerated Prosecco regions (ProseccoDesignationOfOriginQuality — new class), and (4) origin from fresh grapes (FreshGrapeOriginQuality, reused from TBox). The slight deduction (0.05) reflects that the four specific PDO region names ('Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', 'Asolo — Prosecco') are bundled under a single quality class rather than enumerated as separate named individuals, which is an acceptable ontological simplification but does not capture the individual region names as distinct OWL individuals in the TBox.
